flashcards.front: What does RGB stand for and what type of color model is it?
flashcards.back: RGB stands for Red, Green, Blue. It is an additive color model used for light-emitting devices.

flashcards.front: What are the three components of the HSI color model?
flashcards.back: Hue (color type), Saturation (color purity), and Intensity (brightness).

flashcards.front: Why is the CMYK model used in printing instead of CMY?
flashcards.back: CMYK adds black (K) to reduce ink consumption, improve shadow details, and produce better text printing.

flashcards.front: What is the main advantage of YCbCr color model?
flashcards.back: It separates luminance (Y) from chrominance (Cb, Cr), making it efficient for image compression and transmission.

flashcards.front: What does gamut refer to in color models?
flashcards.back: Gamut refers to the range of colors that can be reproduced by a device or represented by a color model.
